rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and attach any generated annex only to the correct owner scope for a component implementation.
Implementation-scope content must remain within the implementation and should not alter subcomponents, connections, features, or properties.
Rely on the input AADL model as the authority for implementation ownership, existing declarations, and structural context before fusion.
generic_rules: Insert content only into the matched implementation owner and keep all unrelated architecture text unchanged.
Preserve declaration order and place implementation-scoped annex content after the structural elements it depends on when scope matters.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use valid AADL annex block form when inserting an annex into an implementation.
Do not move implementation-scoped content into a component type or vice versa.
anti_patterns: Do not create, rename, or delete architectural elements while performing fusion.
Do not infer a new owner scope from requirement wording alone; use the model-resolved target.
Do not introduce references that are not already valid in the chosen owner scope.
irrelevant_topics: Boolean expression examples unrelated to owner/scope fusion decisions.
Validator-driven numeric/boolean/event repair guidance not needed when no diagnostics are active.
